name: LNG Terminal DCS / Safety System Attack
description: Compromise the Distributed Control System and Safety Instrumented System of a Liquefied Natural Gas terminal to cause process upset, overpressure conditions, or disable emergency shutdown — threatening an explosion or environmental release.
root_objective: Attack LNG terminal DCS/SIS to cause overpressure or disable emergency shutdown

nodes.title: Cause LNG Terminal Overpressure or Disable Emergency Shutdown
nodes.description: Compromise the DCS and SIS of an LNG regasification or liquefaction terminal to cause cryogenic overpressure, boil-off gas accumulation, or LNG spill by disabling safety interlocks and manipulating process controls.

nodes.title: Compromise Process Control DCS
nodes.description: Gain access to the Yokogawa CENTUM VP, Honeywell Experion, or ABB 800xA DCS controlling LNG process parameters — tank pressures, cryogenic temperatures, and regasification rates.

nodes.title: Neutralise Safety Instrumented System
nodes.description: Disable or manipulate the independent SIS (Triconex, HIMA, ProSafe-RS) to prevent emergency shutdown from activating when the DCS attack causes process deviation.

nodes.title: Compromise Vendor Remote Maintenance Channel
nodes.description: Exploit the vendor's secure remote access (e.g., Yokogawa Harmony VPN, Honeywell Secure Connection) used for DCS patch management and support.

nodes.title: Pivot from Business Network via Historian
nodes.description: Compromise the data historian server that collects process data from the DCS and feeds it to the business network for reporting and optimization.

nodes.title: Manipulate LNG Tank Level and Pressure Readings
nodes.description: Modify DCS display values to show normal tank levels while altering control valve positions to cause overfill or underpressure conditions in cryogenic storage tanks.

nodes.title: Access SIS Engineering Workstation
nodes.description: Compromise the dedicated SIS engineering workstation that connects to the safety controller, typically isolated but connected during maintenance periods.

nodes.title: Upload Modified Safety Logic to SIS Controller
nodes.description: Modify SIS logic to raise ESD trip setpoints beyond safe limits or add time delays that prevent rapid shutdown, keeping the SIS from intervening during a DCS-initiated process upset.

nodes.title: Disable Boil-Off Gas Compressors
nodes.description: Stop the boil-off gas (BOG) compressors via DCS, causing pressure build-up in LNG storage tanks as natural gas escapes from the cryogenic liquid.

nodes.title: SIS Programming Tool and Credentials
nodes.description: Attacker must obtain the vendor-specific SIS programming software (TriStation, SILworX) and valid configuration passwords for the safety controller.

nodes.title: Vendor Remote Access Remains Persistently Connected
nodes.description: The vendor's remote maintenance VPN tunnel remains active 24/7 for support convenience rather than being activated only during scheduled maintenance windows.

nodes.title: Historian Server Bridges Business and DCS Networks
nodes.description: The process data historian (PI System, IP21) has network interfaces on both the business DMZ and the DCS control network for real-time data collection.
